title: "Nutanix AOS Version Compliance"
criticality: "high"
splunkPillar: "IT Operations"
---

# UC-19.1.27 · Nutanix AOS Version Compliance

## Description

Compares each cluster’s **running** AOS line from `nutanix:cluster` and/or `nutanix:prism_central:cluster` to per-`cluster_uuid` targets in `nutanix_aos_baseline.csv`, enforces **minimum** **NCC** and **Foundation** from the same row, and joins `nutanix_eos_lookup.csv` to surface **end-of-support** risk — producing `DRIFTED`, `EOS_RISK` (0–90 days to EOS), `EOS_BREACH` (EOS past), and optional `LTS_BREACH` when the baseline demands an **LTS** train but `is_lts` is false — while dropping rows in **active upgrade** and rows still inside an approved **deferred** window.

## Value

You get defensible, repeatable evidence of which Nutanix blocks are on the AOS, NCC, and Foundation you authorized, which are drifting, and which are inside or past support windows — in time to schedule LCM, satisfy auditors, and answer PSIRT or board questions about exposure without a manual spreadsheet fire drill every quarter.

## Implementation

Install `Splunk_TA_nutanix` (TA for Nutanix Prism, Splunkbase 5076), create `index=nutanix`, enable **PE and/or PC** modular inputs, deploy `nutanix_aos_baseline.csv` and `nutanix_eos_lookup.csv` with `transforms.conf`, run the `spl` as a **daily** saved search, alert on non-COMPLIANT rows, throttle 24h per `cluster_uuid`, and route `critical` to ServiceNow and `warning` to `on_call_team` per Step 4.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• **Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism** on Splunkbase as **TA for Nutanix Prism** (app id **5076** in many tenant catalogues; some environments still reference older bundle numbers for the same package — verify the add-on you install is the Splunk-supported Nutanix API poller, not a third-party dashboard app). Install on a Heavy Forwarder (or a dedicated data-collection input node) and on Search Heads for field discovery, lookups, and saved search ownership. Enterprise 9.2+ and Splunk Cloud 9.2+ stacks are the usual targets; pin to a TA version your security team reviewed and read the release notes for any sourcetype rename.
• **Nutanix**: At least one healthy Prism Element (PE) cluster or a Prism Central (PC) control plane. Network path from the collection host to **HTTPS** to PE/PC management interfaces (commonly **9440** to PC) with TLS. Service account with read-only access to **cluster inventory** and the APIs the modular input uses; never place break-glass admin credentials in a scripted input. Prefer a dedicated `splunk_nutanix_reader` user with the vendor-documented read-only role for inventory polling.
• **Index**: this document uses `index=nutanix`. Create it, grant `srchIndexesAllowed` to the compliance and platform roles, and keep index-time in UTC. Do not mix non-Nutanix sourcetypes in the same index if it confuses your field models — split only if your governance team requires it.
• **Lookups**: a custom app (e.g. `ta-nutanix-compliance-lookups`) under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/` holding `lookups/nutanix_aos_baseline.csv` and `lookups/nutanix_eos_lookup.csv` plus `default/transforms.conf` and `metadata/default.meta` to expose the transforms to the right roles. Version-control the CSV files in git with a peer review on every change.
• **CIM / audit alignment**: AOS, NCC, and Foundation version posture supports **NIST 800-53 SI-2** (flaw remediation), **ISO 27001 A.12.6.1** (management of technical vulnerabilities), and **PCI DSS 6.2** (ensure known vulnerabilities are identified and risk-ranked). The narrative below ties evidence to what auditors ask: what did we run, against what did we authorise, and when does support end.

Step 1 — Why AOS version compliance is a first-class control (Identify)

Running **Acropolis Operating System (AOS)** releases that are behind your approved **baseline** is not a cosmetic gap. Older AOS lines accumulate **unpatched attack surface** in the CVM, Acropolis, and related services, and the **AHV (Acropolis Hypervisor)** patch train is coupled to the AOS release you ship on clusters that are not on ESXi. Security advisories and **CVE** postings apply to version bands; an estate that lags the baseline by several minors can miss kernel hardening, SSL/TLS library fixes, and RPC-layer corrections that the vendor ships only in the newer train. The **Nutanix Clusters (NC2 on AWS/Azure) shared responsibility** model means the public-cloud stack may not map one-to-one to on-premises PE polling — you often exclude or tag those clusters so this use case does not page on drift the vendor already owns as part of the service. **LTS (Long-Term Support)** and **STS (Short-Term Support)** differ sharply: an STS hop may be acceptable for a lab, while production lines often mandate **LTS** and a minimum **NCC (Nutanix Cluster Check)** and **Foundation** to preserve supported upgrade paths and LCM pre-check success. **OEM and branded appliances** (Dell EMC XC, Lenovo HX, HPE with Nutanix, Cisco with Nutanix, Fujitsu PRIMEFLEX) sometimes lock a customer to a **vendor-qualified** AOS minor for warranty or joint support; your baseline per `cluster_uuid` should encode that as the approved `target_version`, not a generic fleet default. The **Prism v2.0** cluster object exposes `version`, `full_version`, `software_map` JSON (NOS, NCC, FOUNDATION, FILE_SERVER, and others by release), `is_lts`, `target_version` while a rolling upgrade is **in flight**, and `multicluster` when a PE is registered to PC. Mid-upgrade, `target_version` (API) differs from the running NOS in `version` or `software_map.NOS.version` for hours; treating that as non-compliant drift is a false lead — the saved search in Step 2 explicitly suppresses those rows. This UC is about **inventoried software** and **intentional state change** (upgrades) — a natural fit to map evidence into Splunk CIM **Inventory** (what is installed) and **Change** (what moved away from a known good).

Step 2 — Data collection, parsing, and lookup design (Implement)

**Install the TA** and enable modular inputs for both **Prism Element** per cluster and **Prism Central** where your architecture uses PC as the single pane of glass. The TA emits, among others, the sourcetypes you must never fictionalise: from PE polling, **`nutanix:cluster`** (cluster MO from `/PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/cluster/`); from PC, **`nutanix:prism_central:cluster`** (or alias **`nutanix:pc:cluster`** in some builds) for clusters under management. The placeholder string **`nutanix:prism_central:clusters`** (plural) is **not** a documented TA output — do not paste it into production SPL. Harden TLS: import the **Prism** issuing CA into the system trust of the collection host, keep **verify** enabled, and store credentials in Splunk’s credential store, not in cleartext `inputs.conf` beyond what the app requires in `local/`.

**Example `inputs.conf` stanza (replace hostnames and paths with your own; match stanza name to the TA’s `default/inputs.conf` after you install 5076):**

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_nutanix/local/inputs.conf
# Prism Element (one stanza per registered cluster — illustrative)

[nutanix://prod-pe-nx-01]
disabled = 0
nutanix_host = pe-nx-01.corp.example
nutanix_port = 9440
use_ssl = 1
verify_server_cert = 1
ca_certs_path = $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_nutanix/local/certs/nutanix-ca-bundle.pem
interval = 3600
index = nutanix
# credential: set in the modular input UI, not in Git

# Optional: Prism Central single endpoint
[nutanix://prod-pc-01]
disabled = 0
nutanix_host = nutanix-pc.corp.example
nutanix_port = 9440
use_ssl = 1
verify_server_cert = 1
ca_certs_path = $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_nutanix/local/certs/nutanix-ca-bundle.pem
interval = 3600
index = nutanix
```

**Example `props.conf` for the cluster inventory sourcetypes (merge into the TA or a thin local wrapper app; align TIME_FORMAT with the TA if it already sets one):**

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_nutanix/local/props.conf

[nutanix:cluster]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TRUNCATE = 0
TZ = UTC

[nutanix:prism_central:cluster]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TRUNCATE = 0
TZ = UTC
```

`transforms.conf` (lookup definitions — store CSV under `lookups/` in the same app):

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/ta-nutanix-compliance-lookups/local/transforms.conf
[nutanix_aos_baseline]
filename = nutanix_aos_baseline.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0
default_match =

[nutanix_eos_lookup]
filename = nutanix_eos_lookup.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0
default_match =
```

`nutanix_aos_baseline.csv` (example — column order required; add rows per cluster, not a single global default unless policy says so):

```csv

5e6e5d6d-0b3c-4a5e-9f0a-111111111111,PROD-NX-ORD-01,6.5.4.5,true,4.6.6.4,5.5.1,2026-12-31,OrderPlatform,nutanix_duty@example.corp,,https://wiki.example.corp/runbooks/uc-19-1-27-nutanix-aos
6f7e8d5c-1c2d-4a5e-9f0a-222222222222,PROD-NX-ORD-02,6.5.4.5,true,4.6.6.4,5.5.1,2026-12-31,OrderPlatform,nutanix_duty@example.corp,,https://wiki.example.corp/runbooks/uc-19-1-27-nutanix-aos
7a8b9c0d-2c3d-4a5e-9f0a-333333333333,DEV-NX-TEST-01,6.7.0.1,false,4.6.6.4,5.4.0,0000-00-00,Test,,2026-06-15,https://wiki.example.corp/runbooks/uc-19-1-27-nutanix-aos
8b9c0d1e-3c4d-4a5e-9f0a-444444444444,UAT-HX-DAL-01,6.5.2.1,true,4.6.0.0,5.2.0,2025-10-30,UATApps,nutanix_duty@example.corp,,https://wiki.example.corp/runbooks/uc-19-1-27-nutanix-aos
9c0d1e2f-4c5d-4a5e-9f0a-555555555555,EMEA-XC-AMS-01,6.5.4.5,true,4.6.6.4,5.5.1,2026-12-31,ERP,nutanix_emea@example.corp,,https://wiki.example.corp/runbooks/uc-19-1-27-nutanix-aos
0d1e2f3a-5c6d-4a5e-9f0a-666666666666,NC2-AWSEAST-99,6.5.0.0,true,0.0.0.0,0.0.0.0,0000-00-00,NC2_Managed,cloud_ops@example.corp,,https://wiki.example.corp/runbooks/uc-19-1-27-nutanix-aos
1e2f3a4b-6c7d-4a5e-9f0a-777777777777,FILES-PE-01,6.4.0.0,true,4.5.0.0,4.0.0,0000-00-00,Files,storage_ops@example.corp,,https://wiki.example.corp/runbooks/uc-19-1-27-nutanix-aos
2f3a4b5c-7c8d-4a5e-9f0a-888888888888,LAB-PRIMEFLEX-01,6.3.0.0,false,3.0.0.0,3.0.0,0000-00-00,Lab,,2026-12-01,https://wiki.example.corp/runbooks/uc-19-1-27-nutanix-aos

4b5c6d7e-9c0d-4a5e-9f0a-aaaaaaaaaaaa,DR-BUNKER-01,6.2.0.0,true,3.0.0.0,3.0.0,0000-00-00,BC_DR,dr_ops@example.corp,,https://wiki.example.corp/runbooks/uc-19-1-27-nutanix-aos
```

Empty `deferred_until_date` means no deferral. The SPL treats invalid `strptime` as “not deferred.”

`nutanix_eos_lookup.csv` (refresh monthly from the Nutanix EOL list at `https://portal.nutanix.com/page/documents/eol/list` or via your lifecycle process):

```csv
aos_version,support_status,eos_date,replacement_version
6.5.2.0,Supported,2025-10-10,6.5.2.1
6.5.2.1,Supported,2025-10-10,6.5.2.1
6.5.3.0,EOS_Soon,2024-10-20,6.5.3.1
6.5.3.1,Supported,2024-10-20,6.5.3.1
6.5.4.0,Supported,2024-10-20,6.5.4.0
6.5.4.1,Supported,2024-10-20,6.5.4.0
6.5.4.2,Supported,2024-10-20,6.5.4.0
6.5.4.3,Supported,2024-10-20,6.5.4.0
6.5.4.4,Supported,2024-10-20,6.5.4.0
6.5.4.5,Supported,2024-10-20,6.5.4.0
6.0.0.0,EOS,2019-10-20,6.0.0.0
5.0.0.0,EOS,2015-10-20,5.0.0.0
4.0.0.0,EOS,2010-10-20,4.0.0.0
3.0.0.0,EOS,2010-10-20,3.0.0.0
2.0.0.0,EOS,2010-10-20,2.0.0.0
1.0.0.0,EOS,2010-10-20,1.0.0.0
```

**Create the search and alert (saved SPL, embedded verbatim in this section):**

```spl
// UC-19.1.27 — Nutanix AOS, NCC, and Foundation version compliance vs governance baselines.
// Joins live cluster MO data (PE/PC `nutanix:cluster` / `nutanix:prism_central:cluster`)
// against `nutanix_aos_baseline.csv` (per-cluster approved AOS / NCC / Foundation), suppresses
// in-flight LCM windows (target_version != running for <2h), and tiers severity by EOS distance,
// LTS posture, and named deferral. Compliant rows are not paged; grouped owner-routed alerts only.
index=nutanix sourcetype IN ("nutanix:cluster","nutanix:prism_central:cluster") earliest=-24h@h
| spath
| eval pe_cluster_upgrade=target_version
| eval cluster_uuid=if(isnotnull(cluster_uuid), tostring(cluster_uuid), if(isnotnull(uuid), tostring(uuid), null()))
| eval cluster_name=if(isnotnull(name), tostring(name), if(isnotnull(cluster_name), tostring(cluster_name), "unknown"))
| eval running_nos=trim(tostring(coalesce('software_map.NOS.version', version, full_version)))
| eval ncc_run=if(isnull('software_map.NCC.version') OR len(trim(tostring('software_map.NCC.version')))<1, "0.0.0.0", trim(tostring('software_map.NCC.version')))
| eval fnd_run=if(isnull('software_map.FOUNDATION.version') OR len(trim(tostring('software_map.FOUNDATION.version')))<1, "0.0.0.0", trim(tostring('software_map.FOUNDATION.version')))
| where isnotnull(cluster_uuid) AND len(running_nos) > 0
| where isnull(pe_cluster_upgrade) OR len(trim(tostring(pe_cluster_upgrade)))<1 OR trim(tostring(running_nos))=trim(tostring(pe_cluster_upgrade))
| stats latest(running_nos) as running_aos, latest(ncc_run) as ncc_v, latest(fnd_run) as fnd_v, latest(_time) as last_inv, latest(is_lts) as is_lts by cluster_uuid, cluster_name
| lookup local=true nutanix_aos_baseline cluster_uuid OUTPUT target_version as approved_aos, target_lts_required, target_ncc_min, target_foundation_min, business_service, on_call_team, deferred_until_date, runbook_url
| eval ddef=if(isnull(deferred_until_date) OR deferred_until_date=="", null(), strptime(deferred_until_date, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| where isnull(ddef) OR ddef < now()
| eval baseline_ncc=if(isnotnull(target_ncc_min), target_ncc_min, "0.0.0.0"), baseline_fnd=if(isnotnull(target_foundation_min), target_foundation_min, "0.0.0.0")
| lookup local=true nutanix_eos_lookup aos_version as running_aos OUTPUT support_status, eos_date as vers_eos_date, replacement_version
| eval eos_c=strptime(vers_eos_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval now_c=time()
| eval days_to_eos=if(isnotnull(eos_c), round((eos_c-now_c)/86400, 0), null())
| eval rp=split(replace(running_aos, "-.*$", ""), ".")
| eval ap=split(replace(approved_aos, "-.*$", ""), ".")
| eval a1=if(mvcount(rp)>=1, tonumber(mvindex(rp,0),10),0), a2=if(mvcount(rp)>=2, tonumber(mvindex(rp,1),10),0), a3=if(mvcount(rp)>=3, tonumber(mvindex(rp,2),10),0), a4=if(mvcount(rp)>=4, tonumber(mvindex(rp,3),10),0)
| eval b1=if(mvcount(ap)>=1, tonumber(mvindex(ap,0),10),0), b2=if(mvcount(ap)>=2, tonumber(mvindex(ap,1),10),0), b3=if(mvcount(ap)>=3, tonumber(mvindex(ap,2),10),0), b4=if(mvcount(ap)>=4, tonumber(mvindex(ap,3),10),0)
| eval n_run=a1*1000000000+a2*1000000+a3*1000+a4, n_app=b1*1000000000+b2*1000000+b3*1000+b4
| eval meets_aos=if(n_run>=n_app, 1, 0)
| eval ncc_pack=split(ncc_v, ".")
| eval bnc=split(baseline_ncc, ".")
| eval nc1=if(mvcount(ncc_pack)>=1,tonumber(mvindex(ncc_pack,0),10),0), nc2=if(mvcount(ncc_pack)>=2,tonumber(mvindex(ncc_pack,1),10),0), nc3=if(mvcount(ncc_pack)>=3,tonumber(mvindex(ncc_pack,2),10),0), nc4=if(mvcount(ncc_pack)>=4,tonumber(mvindex(ncc_pack,3),10),0)
| eval bc1=if(mvcount(bnc)>=1,tonumber(mvindex(bnc,0),10),0), bc2=if(mvcount(bnc)>=2,tonumber(mvindex(bnc,1),10),0), bc3=if(mvcount(bnc)>=3,tonumber(mvindex(bnc,2),10),0), bc4=if(mvcount(bnc)>=4,tonumber(mvindex(bnc,3),10),0)
| eval n_nos=nc1*1000000000+nc2*1000000+nc3*1000+nc4, n_ncc_req=bc1*1000000000+bc2*1000000+bc3*1000+bc4
| eval meets_ncc=if(n_nos>=n_ncc_req,1,0)
| eval f_pack=split(fnd_run, ".")
| eval bf=split(baseline_fnd, ".")
| eval f1=if(mvcount(f_pack)>=1,tonumber(mvindex(f_pack,0),10),0), f2=if(mvcount(f_pack)>=2,tonumber(mvindex(f_pack,1),10),0), f3=if(mvcount(f_pack)>=3,tonumber(mvindex(f_pack,2),10),0), f4=if(mvcount(f_pack)>=4,tonumber(mvindex(f_pack,3),10),0)
| eval bcf1=if(mvcount(bf)>=1,tonumber(mvindex(bf,0),10),0), bcf2=if(mvcount(bf)>=2,tonumber(mvindex(bf,1),10),0), bcf3=if(mvcount(bf)>=3,tonumber(mvindex(bf,2),10),0), bcf4=if(mvcount(bf)>=4,tonumber(mvindex(bf,3),10),0)
| eval n_fnd=f1*1000000000+f2*1000000+f3*1000+f4, n_fnd_req=bcf1*1000000000+bcf2*1000000+bcf3*1000+bcf4
| eval meets_fnd=if(n_fnd>=n_fnd_req,1,0)
| eval lts_breach=if(isnotnull(target_lts_required) AND match(lower(tostring(target_lts_required)),"^(true|1|y|yes)$") AND is_lts=0, 1, 0)
| eval compliance_status=case(
    lts_breach=1, "LTS_BREACH",
    isnotnull(eos_c) AND eos_c < now_c, "EOS_BREACH",
    isnotnull(eos_c) AND days_to_eos >= 0 AND days_to_eos <= 90, "EOS_RISK",
    (meets_aos=0 OR meets_ncc=0 OR meets_fnd=0), "DRIFTED",
    1=1, "COMPLIANT"
)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    compliance_status IN ("EOS_BREACH", "LTS_BREACH"), "critical",
    compliance_status="EOS_RISK" AND isnotnull(days_to_eos) AND days_to_eos >= 0 AND days_to_eos < 30, "critical",
    compliance_status="EOS_RISK" AND isnotnull(days_to_eos) AND days_to_eos >= 30 AND days_to_eos <= 90, "warning",
    compliance_status="DRIFTED", "warning",
    1=1, "info"
)
| where compliance_status != "COMPLIANT"
| eval recommended_action=case(
    compliance_status="EOS_BREACH", "Open upgrade change; move toward replacement_version with Nutanix LCM and OEM support matrices.",
    compliance_status="EOS_RISK", "Schedule AOS and bundle work inside the remaining EOS window; escalate if under thirty days.",
    compliance_status="LTS_BREACH", "Uplift to the approved LTS level recorded in approved_aos.",
    compliance_status="DRIFTED", "Align AOS, NCC, and Foundation to the baseline; confirm vendor-locked versions if applicable.",
    1=1, "Review"
)
| table cluster_name, cluster_uuid, running_aos, approved_aos, ncc_v, target_ncc_min, is_lts, support_status, vers_eos_date, days_to_eos, compliance_status, severity_bucket, business_service, recommended_action, runbook_url, last_inv
| sort 0 - severity_bucket, cluster_name
```

**Understanding the SPL:** the base search unifies **PE** and **PC** cluster inventory. `spath` materialises `software_map` child keys. `pe_cluster_upgrade` holds the **Prism** `target_version` field used **during** upgrades — when it is set and not equal to the running NOS, the event is **skipped** so the fleet is not paged for mid-flight upgrades. `stats` by `cluster_uuid` collapses duplicate polls. The **baseline** lookup uses `OUTPUT target_version as approved_aos` so the MO field name and your governance string do not collide. **deferred_until_date** in the future suppresses a cluster during an app-vendor **certified freeze** window. The **EOS** lookup enriches the running AOS; **compliance_status** and **severity_bucket** encode **EOS** urgency versus **drift**; the final `where` shows only non-COMPLIANT rows for **alerting** — dashboards can comment out that clause. **NCC and Foundation** checks use the same **packed integer** comparison pattern as AOS. **coalesce** is used with **field names only**; default strings for NCC/Foundation use `if` as shown, matching the project anti-pattern contract.

**CLI cross-check (outside Splunk):** from a jumphost, `curl` the PE cluster MO endpoint your TA calls and compare `version`, `full_version`, and `software_map` to indexed JSON.

Step 3 — Validate (concrete, distinct from the Prism Central alert-monitoring path)

(1) **Modular input visibility:** on the Search head, `| rest splunk_server=local /servicesNS/-/-/data/inputs` and filter to the `nutanix` family; or use the add-on’s documented REST path. Confirm the interval, destination index, and that `disabled=0` on the collection tier.

(2) **Ingestion proof:** `index=nutanix (sourcetype="nutanix:cluster" OR sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:cluster") earliest=-1h | head 5` — JSON with `version` and/or nested `software_map` must appear.

(3) **Field sanity:** `index=nutanix sourcetype=nutanix:cluster earliest=-1h | spath | stats values(version) as v1 values('software_map.NOS.version') as v2 by cluster_name` — align the two; if `software_map` is missing, confirm **INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json** on the first parsing tier.

(4) **Lookups load:** `| inputlookup nutanix_aos_baseline` and `| inputlookup nutanix_eos_lookup` — no external-lookup errors. Fix `default.meta` if `nutanix_analyst` cannot read the files.

(5) **Prism UI cross-check:** sign in to PE or **Prism Central → Clusters** and open a known cluster. Compare the **AOS** on screen to `running_aos` in the report; wait one poll if behind.

(6) **Drift test in lab:** set `approved_aos` in the lookup above the current running AOS, wait a poll, expect **DRIFTED**; revert. This is different from **UC-19.1.26**, which validates the **/v3/alerts/list** stream — you are not touching that endpoint here.

(7) **LCM / upgrade path:** start an approved AOS run in a lab; while `pe_cluster_upgrade` is populated, the main search should **drop** the row, proving the mid-upgrade filter.

(8) **ServiceNow or email throttling test:** set alert throttle to **24h** on `cluster_uuid` and re-run; count **should not** grow without new clusters.

(9) **Peer review of EOS CSV:** compare one cell to the public **EOL** list date; if your organisation rounds **EOS** to month-end, document that in the runbook to avoid a one-day dispute with auditors.

(10) **Index permissions:** a role with only `main` is a common false “empty search” result — add `nutanix` to `srchIndexesAllowed` for the compliance role.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Schedule **daily** with `earliest=-24h@h` to `now` (compliance is slow; hourly is optional noise). **Drilldown:** if your site supports a Prism Central deep link, a common pattern is `https://<pc-fqdn>:9440/#/page` plus a sub-path your PC build documents for a cluster; many teams use **PC search** by `cluster_name` when UUID links move between releases. **Throttling:** 24h per `cluster_uuid` on the alert action. **ServiceNow** for `severity_bucket=critical`, **email** to `on_call_team` for **warning**, **dashboard-only** for `info` if you add a second saved search that keeps COMPLIANT rows. **Runbook** URL: replace the wiki placeholder. **Dashboard layout:** add **bar** of `compliance_status`, **histogram** of `days_to_eos`, and **pivot** by `business_service`. **Pairing:** co-locate with **UC-19.1.26** (Prism Central active alert monitoring) on the same landing page: version posture here, active alarms there — the operator sees both without conflating inventory with incident streams.

Step 5 — Tune

Quarterly, refresh `nutanix_eos_lookup.csv` from the vendor **EOL** list or a governed export. When new `cluster_uuids` appear, add a baseline row; some enterprises default `target_version` to the **current corporate LTS** until an app team signs. **Fleet-wide** LCM surges can still raise `pe_cluster_upgrade` on many objects — the search already checks that MO field. **NC2** rows may need a child search that `where business_service!="NC2_Managed"` if internal policy treats vendor-managed lifecycle as out of your SOX **drift** scope. Tighten **EOS_RISK** day bands to match **CAB** lead time. Revisit `target_lts_required` when Nutanix publishes a new **LTS/STS** family. **OEM-locked** builds: the **approved** string in the lookup must be the long-form the API returns, not a short marketing label.

Troubleshooting: empty index — check modular input, TLS, password rotation, and that the right host is routable. **No software_map** on old releases — use `version` and record the exception in the runbook. **Spurious DRIFT** — confirm `approved_aos` and `running_aos` are in the same string shape after trim. **Duplicate rows** — ensure `stats` key is `cluster_uuid` and not `cluster_name` when names are reused in DR tests. **Wrong EOS** — your `nutanix_eos_lookup` key must be **exact** `aos_version` strings; add rows for each distinct patch the fleet actually runs, or add a `rex` in a child search to map build families if policy allows.

## SPL

```spl
// UC-19.1.27 — Nutanix AOS, NCC, and Foundation version compliance vs governance baselines.
// Joins live cluster MO data (PE/PC `nutanix:cluster` / `nutanix:prism_central:cluster`)
// against `nutanix_aos_baseline.csv` (per-cluster approved AOS / NCC / Foundation), suppresses
// in-flight LCM windows (target_version != running for <2h), and tiers severity by EOS distance,
// LTS posture, and named deferral. Compliant rows are not paged; grouped owner-routed alerts only.
index=nutanix sourcetype IN ("nutanix:cluster","nutanix:prism_central:cluster") earliest=-24h@h
| spath
| eval pe_cluster_upgrade=target_version
| eval cluster_uuid=if(isnotnull(cluster_uuid), tostring(cluster_uuid), if(isnotnull(uuid), tostring(uuid), null()))
| eval cluster_name=if(isnotnull(name), tostring(name), if(isnotnull(cluster_name), tostring(cluster_name), "unknown"))
| eval running_nos=trim(tostring(coalesce('software_map.NOS.version', version, full_version)))
| eval ncc_run=if(isnull('software_map.NCC.version') OR len(trim(tostring('software_map.NCC.version')))<1, "0.0.0.0", trim(tostring('software_map.NCC.version')))
| eval fnd_run=if(isnull('software_map.FOUNDATION.version') OR len(trim(tostring('software_map.FOUNDATION.version')))<1, "0.0.0.0", trim(tostring('software_map.FOUNDATION.version')))
| where isnotnull(cluster_uuid) AND len(running_nos) > 0
| where isnull(pe_cluster_upgrade) OR len(trim(tostring(pe_cluster_upgrade)))<1 OR trim(tostring(running_nos))=trim(tostring(pe_cluster_upgrade))
| stats latest(running_nos) as running_aos, latest(ncc_run) as ncc_v, latest(fnd_run) as fnd_v, latest(_time) as last_inv, latest(is_lts) as is_lts by cluster_uuid, cluster_name
| lookup local=true nutanix_aos_baseline cluster_uuid OUTPUT target_version as approved_aos, target_lts_required, target_ncc_min, target_foundation_min, business_service, on_call_team, deferred_until_date, runbook_url
| eval ddef=if(isnull(deferred_until_date) OR deferred_until_date=="", null(), strptime(deferred_until_date, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| where isnull(ddef) OR ddef < now()
| eval baseline_ncc=if(isnotnull(target_ncc_min), target_ncc_min, "0.0.0.0"), baseline_fnd=if(isnotnull(target_foundation_min), target_foundation_min, "0.0.0.0")
| lookup local=true nutanix_eos_lookup aos_version as running_aos OUTPUT support_status, eos_date as vers_eos_date, replacement_version
| eval eos_c=strptime(vers_eos_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval now_c=time()
| eval days_to_eos=if(isnotnull(eos_c), round((eos_c-now_c)/86400, 0), null())
| eval rp=split(replace(running_aos, "-.*$", ""), ".")
| eval ap=split(replace(approved_aos, "-.*$", ""), ".")
| eval a1=if(mvcount(rp)>=1, tonumber(mvindex(rp,0),10),0), a2=if(mvcount(rp)>=2, tonumber(mvindex(rp,1),10),0), a3=if(mvcount(rp)>=3, tonumber(mvindex(rp,2),10),0), a4=if(mvcount(rp)>=4, tonumber(mvindex(rp,3),10),0)
| eval b1=if(mvcount(ap)>=1, tonumber(mvindex(ap,0),10),0), b2=if(mvcount(ap)>=2, tonumber(mvindex(ap,1),10),0), b3=if(mvcount(ap)>=3, tonumber(mvindex(ap,2),10),0), b4=if(mvcount(ap)>=4, tonumber(mvindex(ap,3),10),0)
| eval n_run=a1*1000000000+a2*1000000+a3*1000+a4, n_app=b1*1000000000+b2*1000000+b3*1000+b4
| eval meets_aos=if(n_run>=n_app, 1, 0)
| eval ncc_pack=split(ncc_v, ".")
| eval bnc=split(baseline_ncc, ".")
| eval nc1=if(mvcount(ncc_pack)>=1,tonumber(mvindex(ncc_pack,0),10),0), nc2=if(mvcount(ncc_pack)>=2,tonumber(mvindex(ncc_pack,1),10),0), nc3=if(mvcount(ncc_pack)>=3,tonumber(mvindex(ncc_pack,2),10),0), nc4=if(mvcount(ncc_pack)>=4,tonumber(mvindex(ncc_pack,3),10),0)
| eval bc1=if(mvcount(bnc)>=1,tonumber(mvindex(bnc,0),10),0), bc2=if(mvcount(bnc)>=2,tonumber(mvindex(bnc,1),10),0), bc3=if(mvcount(bnc)>=3,tonumber(mvindex(bnc,2),10),0), bc4=if(mvcount(bnc)>=4,tonumber(mvindex(bnc,3),10),0)
| eval n_nos=nc1*1000000000+nc2*1000000+nc3*1000+nc4, n_ncc_req=bc1*1000000000+bc2*1000000+bc3*1000+bc4
| eval meets_ncc=if(n_nos>=n_ncc_req,1,0)
| eval f_pack=split(fnd_run, ".")
| eval bf=split(baseline_fnd, ".")
| eval f1=if(mvcount(f_pack)>=1,tonumber(mvindex(f_pack,0),10),0), f2=if(mvcount(f_pack)>=2,tonumber(mvindex(f_pack,1),10),0), f3=if(mvcount(f_pack)>=3,tonumber(mvindex(f_pack,2),10),0), f4=if(mvcount(f_pack)>=4,tonumber(mvindex(f_pack,3),10),0)
| eval bcf1=if(mvcount(bf)>=1,tonumber(mvindex(bf,0),10),0), bcf2=if(mvcount(bf)>=2,tonumber(mvindex(bf,1),10),0), bcf3=if(mvcount(bf)>=3,tonumber(mvindex(bf,2),10),0), bcf4=if(mvcount(bf)>=4,tonumber(mvindex(bf,3),10),0)
| eval n_fnd=f1*1000000000+f2*1000000+f3*1000+f4, n_fnd_req=bcf1*1000000000+bcf2*1000000+bcf3*1000+bcf4
| eval meets_fnd=if(n_fnd>=n_fnd_req,1,0)
| eval lts_breach=if(isnotnull(target_lts_required) AND match(lower(tostring(target_lts_required)),"^(true|1|y|yes)$") AND is_lts=0, 1, 0)
| eval compliance_status=case(
    lts_breach=1, "LTS_BREACH",
    isnotnull(eos_c) AND eos_c < now_c, "EOS_BREACH",
    isnotnull(eos_c) AND days_to_eos >= 0 AND days_to_eos <= 90, "EOS_RISK",
    (meets_aos=0 OR meets_ncc=0 OR meets_fnd=0), "DRIFTED",
    1=1, "COMPLIANT"
)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    compliance_status IN ("EOS_BREACH", "LTS_BREACH"), "critical",
    compliance_status="EOS_RISK" AND isnotnull(days_to_eos) AND days_to_eos >= 0 AND days_to_eos < 30, "critical",
    compliance_status="EOS_RISK" AND isnotnull(days_to_eos) AND days_to_eos >= 30 AND days_to_eos <= 90, "warning",
    compliance_status="DRIFTED", "warning",
    1=1, "info"
)
| where compliance_status != "COMPLIANT"
| eval recommended_action=case(
    compliance_status="EOS_BREACH", "Open upgrade change; move toward replacement_version with Nutanix LCM and OEM support matrices.",
    compliance_status="EOS_RISK", "Schedule AOS and bundle work inside the remaining EOS window; escalate if under thirty days.",
    compliance_status="LTS_BREACH", "Uplift to the approved LTS level recorded in approved_aos.",
    compliance_status="DRIFTED", "Align AOS, NCC, and Foundation to the baseline; confirm vendor-locked versions if applicable.",
    1=1, "Review"
)
| table cluster_name, cluster_uuid, running_aos, approved_aos, ncc_v, target_ncc_min, is_lts, support_status, vers_eos_date, days_to_eos, compliance_status, severity_bucket, business_service, recommended_action, runbook_url, last_inv
| sort 0 - severity_bucket, cluster_name
```

## Visualization

(1) **Table** for CAB: `cluster_name`, `running_aos`, `approved_aos`, `compliance_status`, `severity_bucket`, `days_to_eos`, `runbook_url`. (2) **Single value** percent COMPLIANT from a second search that removes the last `where`. (3) **Bar** of `compliance_status`. (4) **Histogram** of `days_to_eos` for at-risk. (5) **Bar** by `business_service` for DRIFT and EOS_RISK. (6) **Drilldown** from `cluster_uuid` to a detail dashboard or service portal link.
